2606.20373.title: AutoPass: Evidence-Guided LLM Agents for Compiler Performance Tuning
2606.20373.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise for code compilation tasks, but applying them to runtime performance tuning is difficult due to complex microarchitectural effects and noisy runtime measurements. We present AutoPass, a multi-agent framework for compiler performance tuning that uses compiler and runtime evidence to guide LLM-generated optimization decisions. Rather than treating the compiler as a black box like prior auto-tuning schemes, AutoPass opens up the compiler to the LLM, enabling it to query compiler-internal optimization states and analyze the intermediate representation to orchestrate compiler options. The search process iteratively refines optimization configurations using measured runtime feedback to diagnose regressions and guide latency-improving edits. AutoPass operates in an inference-only, training-free setting and requires no offline training or task-specific fine-tuning, making it readily applicable to new benchmarks and platforms. We implement AutoPass on the LLVM compiler and evaluate it on server-grade x86-64 and embedded ARM64 systems. AutoPass outperforms expert-tuned heuristics and classical autotuning methods, achieving geometric-mean speedups of 1.043x and 1.117x over LLVM -O3 on x86-64 and ARM64, respectively.
2606.20373.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在代碼編譯任務中顯示出潛力，但由於複雜的微架構效應和噪聲的運行時測量，將其應用於運行時性能調優是困難的。我們提出了AutoPass，一個多代理框架，用於編譯器性能調優，利用編譯器和運行時證據來指導LLM生成的優化決策。與以前的自動調優方案將編譯器視為黑箱不同，AutoPass向LLM開放編譯器，使其能夠查詢編譯器內部的優化狀態並分析中間表示，以協調編譯器選項。搜索過程利用測量的運行時反饋迭代地細化優化配置，以診斷回歸並指導改善延遲的編輯。AutoPass在僅推理的、無需訓練的環境中運行，並且不需要離線訓練或特定任務的微調，這使其能夠輕鬆應用於新的基準和平台。我們在LLVM編譯器上實現了AutoPass，並在伺服器級x86-64和嵌入式ARM64系統上進行了評估。AutoPass的性能超過了專家調優的啟發式方法和傳統自動調優方法，在x86-64和ARM64上分別實現了1.043倍和1.117倍的幾何平均加速，相較於LLVM -O3。
2606.20373.author: Zepeng Li et.al.
2606.20373.authors: Zepeng Li, Jie Ren, Zhanyong Tang, Jie Zheng, Zheng Wang
